title: extract_first_url matches URLs inside href attributes of rendered HTML
description: extract_first_url() is called with &text (raw plaintext input). This is correct for the create_status path. However, the regex r"https://[^\s<>\]\)]+" has a subtle issue: if the user's input contains Markdown-style links like [text](https://example.com), the regex will match and include the trailing ) in the URL since \) only excludes literal closing paren at end — actually wait, \) IS in the exclusion class. Looking again: the character class is [^\s<>\]\)] which excludes ], ), <, >, and whitespace. This is actually correct for common cases. Downgrading: the text is plaintext, not HTML, so this is fine. One edge case: if text contains a bare URL with trailing punctuation like https://example.com. (period), the period will be included in the extracted URL. Mastodon strips trailing punctuation (.\!?,;:) from URLs.
